rulings.Case Title: INSTANT WHIP TIP TOP FOODS, INC. VS. PENSABENE INTERNATIONAL, INC., DBA LADY BALTIMORE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Assignment Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 30, 2013, Line 1, ASSIGNEE K ELDER DBA GRANITE FINANCIAL'S Notice Of Motion For Assignment Order Granted. No opposition filed. Upon a noticed motion, the court may order the judgment debtor to assign to the judgment creditor all or part of a right to payment due or to become due, whether or not the right is conditioned on future developments. (CCP § 708.510(a).) CCP § 1540 permits an assignee judgment creditor to recover unclaimed property that previously escheated to the State. (Weingarten Realty Investors v. Chiang (2012) 212 Cal.App.4th 163, 168.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: KIM TIMMONS VS. TRADER JOE'S COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 30, 2013, Line 1. Defendant TRADER JOE'S COMPANY, INC.'S Motion for Terminating Sanctions Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued on the Court's own motion to June 19, 2013. Plaintiff's opposition asserts that all outstanding discovery has been answered, however, the opposition does not contain the responses to Special Interrogatories or verifications to any responses other than the form interrogatories. No later than June 5, 2013, Plaintiff's counsel shall file a supplemental declaration attaching these items and also affirming that all documents that were promised to be produced have in fact been produced. Defendant may file a response to the supplemental declaration no later than June 12, 2013 and address whether terminating sanctions continue to be warranted under the guidelines articulated by the Courts of Appeal (See, e.g., Van Sickle v. Gilbert (2011) 196 Cal. App. 4th 1495 ; Doppes v. Bentley Motors, Inc. (2009) 174 Cal. App. 4th 967) if all outstanding discovery has been answered. Courtesy copies are to be provided to Department 302 on the date of filing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL LONG VS. SAN FRANCISCO 49ERS LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Permitting Confidential Materials To Be Filed Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 30, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANT THE NATIONAL FOOTBALL LEAGUE'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Permitting Confidential Materials To Be Filed Under Seal Granted. No opposition filed and the motion complies with CRC 2.550 et seq. The Court finds that there is an overriding interest in sealing the security documents that benefit the public welfare, that interest will be prejudiced if the information is not sealed, the proposed sealing order is narrowly tailored, and there is no less restrictive means to achieve the overriding interest. The Court will sign an order substantially in the form submitted by the moving party, but it must be revised to specifically list the confidential items and page and line numbers to be sealed that are highlighted in paragraphs 3 - 5 of the declaration of Dana M. Ulise filed in support of the motion shall be sealed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LAUREE PRIETO VS. B&H EDUCATION INC. DBA MARINELLO SCHOOLS OF BEAUTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 30, 2013, Line 23, DEFENDANT B&H EDUCATION, INC. DBA MARINELLO SCHOOLS OF Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions Judge Miller discloses that she is a member of the same Inn of Court as defense counsel Ari Hersher. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. Granted, in part. Within 20 days, Plaintiff shall do the following: Provide verified further responses and produce documents as to Request for Production nos. 2 - 4, 5 - 9, 10 - 26, 28, 31 - 54, 58, 59 - 63, 65-76. If there are document requests where Plaintiff has conducted a reasonably diligent search and has by now produced all responsive, non-privileged documents in her possession, custody or control, she should state so in writing. Plaintiff shall respond to Form Interrogatory - General, no. 2.5 (provide addresses), 8.7, 10.1 - 10.3, Special Interrogatory nos. 1, 10, 14 - 15, 17, 18, 19. 86, Form Interrogatory -Employment nos. 200.4, 209.1, 213.1 - 213.2. As to 213.1 and 213.2, plaintiff shall provide responses, but may include objections, and need not include documents as to which plaintiff is invoking a claim of privilege. The motion to compel is denied as to Form Interrogatory - Employment, no. 205.1, 210.6, 212.4. For any responsive document withheld on the basis privilege, responding party shall serve a privilege log that identifies the nature of the document, date, author, recipients, Bates numbers, and the privilege claimed. Requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Counsel are ordered to continue to meet and confer in good faith about issues that may arise regarding Plaintiff's required responses, as set forth in this Order, so that they can avoid unnecessary motions practice to the extent issues may arise about the subject matter and scope of some of the discovery requests to which, up to this point, Plaintiff has asserted only objections. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the specific portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LAUREE PRIETO VS. B&H EDUCATION INC. DBA MARINELLO SCHOOLS OF BEAUTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deposition Of Pltf And Req. For Sanctions In The Amount Of $5325.00 Against Pltf And Pltf'S Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 30, 2013, Line 23, DEFENDANT B&H EDUCATION, INC. DBA MARINELLO SCHOOLS OF Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $5325.00 Against Plaintiff And Plaintiff'S Counsel. Judge Miller discloses that she is a member of the same Inn of Court as defense counsel Ari Hersher. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. Granted. A non-resident party witness may be deposed in California. (Glass v. Sup.Ct. (1988) 204 Cal.App.3d 1048, 1052 - 1053.) The factors enumerated in CCP § 2025.260 favor compelling Plaintiff to be deposed in California. Plaintiff elected to sue in California. All of the documents, other witnesses and attorneys are in California. Defendant has offered to pay for Plaintiff's expenses and to accommodate Plaintiff's schedule. The deposition shall be limited to two consecutive days. Defendant shall pay for Plaintiff's reasonable expenses and costs to travel to California for the deposition. Requests for monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the specific portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: KELLEY MAZZEI, INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF VS. REGAL ENTERTAINMENT GROUP, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 30, 2013, Line 15, PLAINTIFF KELLEY MAZZEI, INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF OTHER MEMBERS OF THE GENERAL PUBLIC SIMILARLY SITUATED Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Class Action Complaint Plaintiff's motion for leave to file first amended class action complaint is granted. rulings.Case Title: MOHAMMED RIBHI AYESH VS. MICHAEL TIMOTHY BOWEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Ptlf To Respond To Deft'S Discovery Demands, For The Genuiness Of All Documents And The Truth Of All Matters Specified In Bowen'S Requests For Admissions To Be Deemed Admitted, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 30, 2013, Line 3. Defendant MICHAEL BOWEN'S Motion to Compel Plaintiff to Respond to Defendant's Discovery Demands, for the Genuiness of All Documents and The Truth of All Matters Specified in Bowen's Requests for Admissions to be Deemed Admitted, and for Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed; motion granted. Plaintiff shall provide supplemental responses within 21 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WINELAND-THOMSON ADVENTURES, INC. DBA THOMSON SAFARIS VS. DOES 1 THROUGH 10, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moton For Order Of Cotnempt And Sanctions Against Failure To Produce Documents Pursuant To Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 30, 2013, Line 21, PLAINTIFF WINELAND -THOMPSON ADVENTURES, INC. DBA THOMSON'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Of Contempt And Sanctions Against Failure To Produce Documents Pursuant To Subpoena Judge Miller discloses that in summer 2004 she travelled to Tanzania on a Thomson Family Travel trip. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in the case. Judge Miller has no personal knowledge of this case. Off calendar. Discovery is stayed in light of Defendants' filing of the special motion to strike. (CCP § 425.16(g).) The stay on "[a]ll discovery proceedings" applies to discovery motions, "including those already pending at the time the special motion to strike is filed." (Britts v. Sup.Ct. (2006) 145 Cal.App.4th 1112, 1128.) Plaintiff may re-file its motion after the ruling on the special motion to strike. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
